Its classification is a result of the combination of characteristics from its parent species, Epidendrum and Cattleya. The hybrid orchid classification reflects its unique genetic makeup, combining traits from both genera. The classification of × Epicattleya guatemalensis is an example of how hybridization can lead to new and distinct species in the plant kingdom. As a hybrid, × Epicattleya guatemalensis is often considered a separate entity from its parent species in terms of classification and identification.
